symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe left-upper-quadrant pain radiating to the left shoulder

snomed_ref.display: Pain in left upper quadrant of abdomen

symptoms.character: sharp, twisting, unrelenting; worse on movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt severe LUQ pain with shoulder-tip radiation over a massively enlarged spleen is classic for splenic infarction/capsular stretch.
The same enlarged fragile spleen is at risk of catastrophic rupture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Month of left-upper-quadrant dragging fullness with early satiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive LUQ fullness and early satiety are the cardinal patient-reportable symptoms of an enlarging spleen.
Splenomegaly is a hallmark of CML.
Spontaneous atraumatic mucosal bleeding indicates a platelet/coagulation defect.
Marrow infiltration causing thrombocytopenia is a red-flag mechanism for spontaneous bleeding.

symptoms.symptom_name: New easy bruising over the lower limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Atraumatic bruising complements mucosal bleeding as evidence of a platelet disorder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Three months of unintentional weight loss and drenching night sweats (B-symptoms)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional B-symptoms with organomegaly and bleeding strongly suggest a haematological malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive firm tender splenomegaly palpable 8 cm below the left costal margin, crossing the midline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Massive splenomegaly with focal tenderness over the splenic pole supports CML with splenic infarction — but this is a clinician palpation the AI cannot perform.
Sternal tenderness reflects marrow expansion in myeloproliferative/leukaemic disease — a clinician-elicited sign, sealed.
Pallor suggests anaemia from marrow crowding — clinician-observed on examination.
Tachycardia may reflect pain, anaemia or early compromise; a clinician-measured vital sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood examination and blood film

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination with peripheral blood film
investigation_details.expected_result: Marked leukocytosis with a left shift, basophilia and immature myeloid precursors; anaemia and thrombocytopenia; blast percentage to define phase
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis of leukaemia and its phase cannot be established; bleeding and leukostasis risk go unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The blood film pattern plus BCR-ABL1 confirmation is diagnostic — a hospital investigation the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cytogenetics / BCR-ABL1 molecular testing (Philadelphia chromosome t(9;22))

investigation_details.expected_result: t(9;22) Philadelphia chromosome / BCR-ABL1 fusion positive
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: CML cannot be confirmed and TKI therapy cannot be appropriately directed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: BCR-ABL1/t(9;22) positivity defines CML — molecular testing unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal ultrasound / CT of the spleen

investigation_details.expected_result: Massive splenomegaly with wedge-shaped infarction; assessment for capsular breach/rupture and free fluid
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Splenic infarction versus impending/actual rupture cannot be distinguished — a surgical emergency may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is required to characterise the splenic event and exclude rupture — not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No anticoagulant or NSAID use (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of anticoagulant/NSAID makes an iatrogenic cause of the bleeding unlikely, pointing to an intrinsic bleeding diathesis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Undiagnosed haematological malignancy with acute splenic event
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Chronic myeloid leukaemia with splenic infarction and bleeding diathesis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of massive splenic-type pain, spontaneous bleeding, easy bruising and B-symptoms is a haematological emergency pattern that mandates emergency escalation even though the specific diagnosis cannot be made remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency full blood examination, blood film and coagulation profile (in hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies leukocytosis, anaemia, thrombocytopenia and bleeding risk and defines leukaemic phase — but is a hospital test; the AI arranges the pathway, it does not perform it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent abdominal imaging (ultrasound/CT) for the splenic event
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguishes splenic infarction from impending/actual rupture — a potential surgical emergency.
Confirms CML and directs specialist therapy; performed after hospital admission.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home/remote attempt to obtain a definitive diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No telehealth pathway can obtain a blood film, cytogenetics or abdominal imaging; attempting to diagnose or treat remotely delays emergency care.
